skill.name: video-edit
skill.description: Edit existing video on RunComfy — this skill is a smart router that matches the user's intent to the right edit model in the RunComfy catalog. Picks Wan 2.7 Edit-Video (general restyle / background swap / packaging swap, identity + motion preservation), Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control (transfer precise motion from a reference video to a target character), or Lucy Edit Restyle (lightweight identity-stable restyle / outfit swap). Bundles each model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper edits without burning iterations on the wrong model. Calls `runcomfy run <vendor>/<model>/<endpoint>` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "video edit", "edit video", "restyle video", "swap video background", "motion control", "outfit swap video", or any explicit ask to transform a video.
skill.summary: Smart video editing router that selects the optimal AI model for restyle, motion transfer, or identity-stable edits on RunComfy.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is pure markdown documentation for legitimate CLI usage. No malicious code, no command injection vulnerabilities, no data exfiltration. Network calls go to legitimate RunComfy API endpoints. The skill routes users to video editing AI models through the official RunComfy CLI.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill contains 66 references to CLI commands using backtick syntax. These are documented command examples in markdown, not executable code. All commands invoke the official RunComfy CLI for legitimate video editing operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SKILL.md is documentation-only. All backtick patterns are markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples, not Ruby code execution. No dynamic command construction from user input.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: 14 URLs are hardcoded in the documentation. All point to legitimate RunComfy service endpoints and documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All hardcoded URLs are legitimate RunComfy service endpoints and GitHub repository. No malicious C2 infrastructure or exfiltration endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: Path Patterns in Documentation
low_findings.description: Paths like ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json and <absolute/path> appear in documentation. These represent standard config storage and user-specified output directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json is standard Unix config storage. <absolute/path> is a placeholder for user-specified output. No path traversal vulnerability exists.

low_findings.title: ML Seed Parameter Misidentified
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'seed' parameters as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are ML reproducibility parameters, not cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: 'seed' in ML context means deterministic randomness control, not cryptographic seeding. The skill does not use weak cryptographic algorithms.

content.user_title: Edit Videos with AI-Powered Restyle and Motion Control
content.value_statement: Transform existing videos with AI precision. This skill routes your video editing requests to the optimal AI model for restyling, background swaps, motion transfer, or outfit changes while preserving identity.
actual_capabilities: Route video editing requests to the optimal AI model based on intent (restyle, motion transfer, or localized edits)
Execute background swaps on videos while preserving identity, pose, and lip movement
Transfer precise motion from reference videos to target characters with identity hold
Perform lightweight outfit and costume swaps with identity preservation
Apply atmospheric restyling (lighting changes, scene relighting) to video content
Invoke Wan 2.7, Kling 2.6 Pro, or Lucy Edit models through the RunComfy CLI
limitations: Maximum video duration varies by model: Wan (2-10s), Kling (10-30s), Lucy (varies)
Maximum resolution: 1080p for Wan, 720p for Lucy
Cannot blend multiple models in a single operation
No aspect ratio control for Lucy Edit model

use_cases.title: Product Video Background Swap
use_cases.description: Replace the background in a product demonstration video while keeping the speaker, camera motion, and hand movements intact. Upload your video and reference image for precise packaging design replacement.

use_cases.title: Motion Transfer for Animation
use_cases.description: Take a reference video of a dancer and transfer that exact motion to a target character image. The AI preserves the character's identity while applying the reference motion.

use_cases.title: Outfit Swap for Fashion Content
use_cases.description: Change the clothing in an existing video while preserving the subject's face, pose, and overall motion. Lightweight edits perfect for fashion content updates.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Background Swap
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a video at [VIDEO_URL] showing a person talking. Change the background to [NEW_BACKGROUND] while keeping their face, pose, and lip movements the same.

prompt_templates.title: Motion Transfer Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to transfer the motion from [REFERENCE_VIDEO_URL] to the character in [TARGET_IMAGE_URL]. The character should maintain their identity but perform the same movements.

prompt_templates.title: Outfit Change Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the outfit in [VIDEO_URL] to [NEW_OUTFIT_DESCRIPTION]. Preserve the person's face, pose, and overall motion throughout the video.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Element Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to make multiple edits to [VIDEO_URL]: 1) Change background to [DESCRIPTION], 2) Update lighting to [STYLE], 3) Preserve identity and motion. Process each step separately for best results.

output_examples.input: Swap the background in my product video to a modern office with natural lighting
output_examples.output: The skill analyzes your request, selects Wan 2.7 Edit-Video as the optimal model, and generates a video with your original speaker's face, pose, and lip movements preserved against a new modern office background with natural lighting.

output_examples.input: Transfer the dancing motion from this reference video to my character image
output_examples.output: The skill routes to Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control, extracts the motion patterns from your reference, and applies them to your character while maintaining their visual identity. Output is saved to your specified directory.

output_examples.input: Change the outfit in this video from casual to business attire
output_examples.output: The skill selects Lucy Edit Restyle for lightweight identity-preserving edits, processes your video to show the subject in professional business attire while keeping their face and motion intact.

best_practices: Lead your prompts with preservation goals: specify what to keep (face, pose, lip movement) before describing the change
Make one edit direction per request for best results; compound edits can cause motion drift
Use the skill's intent routing to your advantage; let it pick the optimal model rather than forcing a specific one
anti_patterns: Do not request multiple major changes in a single prompt; split into sequential edits instead
Avoid requesting full character replacement or identity swaps; these models preserve identity, not replace it
Do not use low-resolution inputs where the subject occupies less than 5% of the frame; identity hold will be unreliable

faq.question: Which video editing model should I use?
faq.answer: The skill automatically routes to the best model for your intent. Wan 2.7 is the default for restyle and background swaps. Use Kling for motion transfer from reference videos. Use Lucy for lightweight localized edits like outfit changes.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: Most models accept MP4 and MOV formats. Some models like Lucy also support WEBM and GIF. All videos must be under 100MB and meet the duration limits of the specific model.

faq.question: Can I edit videos longer than the limits?
faq.answer: You will need to split longer videos into segments that fit within each model's limits, then stitch the results. The skill processes one segment at a time.

faq.question: How does the skill preserve identity?
faq.answer: The underlying AI models (Wan, Kling, Lucy) are trained for identity preservation. Prompts that start with preservation goals help the model focus on what to keep versus what to change.

faq.question: Is my video data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: Your video URLs are sent to the RunComfy Model API for processing. The skill uses HTTPS and only communicates with RunComfy's servers. Generated outputs are downloaded to your specified directory.

faq.question: Can I use reference images with these models?
faq.answer: Yes. Wan 2.7 supports reference_image for direct design transfer (like packaging swaps). Kling uses reference images for character consistency. Lucy supports reference images for style guidance.
